definition: A structured trail showing the user's location within a site or data hierarchy, as an ordered list of links from root to current page. Answers "where am I?" — distinct from a navigation menu ("where can I go?"), tabs ("which panel?"), or a stepper ("how far through this process?"). Realized as a nav[aria-label] landmark wrapping an ordered list, the current crumb a non-link carrying aria-current="page", with presentational (never announced) separators.
usage: Realized by the Breadcrumb block, which implements the Navigation Intent and composes the Typography Intent for crumb-label truncation. Overflow collapses the middle behind an ellipsis disclosure, keeping root + current visible.
